title: World Food Day: A Global Feast for Good
story: Every October 16, people around the world celebrate World Food Day by sharing meals, learning about hunger, and helping their communities. Schools organize food drives where students bring canned goods for local food banks. Restaurants donate meals to shelters, and families cook traditional dishes from different countries to celebrate food diversity. Social media fills with photos of colorful meals and facts about fighting hunger worldwide. It's a day when everyone can make a difference just by sharing food and caring about others.
activity_question: What do schools often organize on World Food Day?
activity_choices: Food drives for local food banks
activity_answer: Food drives for local food banks
follow_up_question: What's one way your family could help fight hunger in your community?

title: Dictionary Day: Words Worth Celebrating!
story: Every October 16, word lovers everywhere celebrate Dictionary Day by diving into their favorite dictionaries! This special day honors the amazing books that help us understand what words mean. People share their favorite unusual words on social media, teachers organize vocabulary games, and libraries host word-hunting contests. Some families even play dictionary games where they try to guess made-up definitions!
activity_question: What do people share on social media during Dictionary Day?
follow_up_question: What's the most interesting word you've ever discovered in a dictionary?

title: Stand Tall for Your Spine!
story: Your backbone gets its own special day on October 16 during World Spine Day! People around the world do stretches, practice good posture, and learn how to keep their spines healthy and strong. Schools might teach kids proper ways to sit and carry backpacks, while doctors share tips about protecting your back.
activity_question: What do people focus on during World Spine Day?
activity_choices: Learning to keep their spines healthy
Counting how many bones they have
activity_answer: Learning to keep their spines healthy
follow_up_question: What's one thing you do every day that might affect your spine health?
